build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: The player digs rooms by spending gold on soil tiles, creating a dungeon layout. Rooms are placed on a grid and connected by corridors. Digging visibly removes earth and creates passable space. Score 0 if there is no digging mechanic, if rooms are pre-built, or if the player cannot design the layout. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Monsters are recruited by spending gold and placed in rooms. Each monster has an upkeep cost (food) and a combat strength. A creature happiness meter is visible and affected by room suitability. Unhappy creatures eventually desert. Score 0 if monsters cannot be recruited, if there is no happiness system, or if upkeep does not exist. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Heroes invade periodically, entering from the surface and navigating toward treasure rooms. Heroes fight monsters and trigger traps. Defeated heroes drop gold loot; heroes that escape increase dungeon fame attracting stronger parties. Score 0 if no heroes invade, if heroes do not fight monsters, or if there is no consequence for escaped heroes. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

A gold economy drives the dungeon: gold is earned from defeated heroes, spent on digging, monsters, traps, and food. A gold counter is permanently visible. Attempting actions without gold fails clearly. Score 0 if there is no gold system or if everything is free. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

The full loop connects: title -> new dungeon -> dig/recruit/manage -> hero waves -> survive or dungeon heart destroyed -> result screen. The game ends when the dungeon heart falls or a wave milestone is reached. Score 0 if any transition is missing or if there is no end condition. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

At least four monster types exist with different sprites, combat stats, room preferences, and costs. Score 0 if fewer than three types exist or if types are visually identical. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

At least three room types exist (treasure room, trap room, barracks, hatchery) with different visual appearances and functions. Room type affects monster happiness and dungeon strategy. Score 0 if all rooms are identical or if room type has no effect. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Heroes come in at least three types (warrior, mage, rogue) with different sprites, combat approaches, and weaknesses. Later waves bring stronger hero parties. Score 0 if all heroes are identical or if waves do not escalate. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Traps can be placed in corridors and rooms, dealing damage to heroes who trigger them. At least two trap types exist with different effects. Score 0 if no traps exist or if traps have no effect on heroes. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Dungeon fame escalates over time based on escaped heroes, attracting progressively stronger parties. The player must continuously upgrade defenses to keep pace. A fame or threat indicator is visible. Score 0 if difficulty never increases or if there is no escalation system. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

The HUD shows gold, creature count, creature happiness, and wave/fame indicator in clear non-overlapping regions. The player always knows their economic and defensive state. Score 0 if gold, creature info, or wave state is missing. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The dungeon cross-section clearly shows dug vs undug space, room types, monster positions, hero positions, and traps. The surface entrance is visible. Score 0 if rooms are indistinguishable or if heroes and monsters cannot be told apart. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Actions produce visible feedback: digging removes earth with effects, placing monsters shows them appear, heroes entering triggers an alert, combat shows fighting, traps trigger visibly, and loot drops are shown. Score 0 if actions produce no observable change. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Overall art coherence: dungeon walls, rooms, monsters, heroes, traps, and UI belong to a consistent dark-fantasy underground visual language. Score 0 if the screen mixes incompatible styles or reads as plain default Godot grey. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Monster and hero sprites are real illustrated or pixel-art characters with distinct appearances per type. Monsters look menacing; heroes look adventurous. Score 0 if characters are plain colored shapes. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Dungeon walls and floors use real textured tiles (stone, dirt, carved surfaces). Room types have distinct visual treatments (treasure glints, barracks weapons, hatchery eggs). Score 0 if walls are uniform colored fills. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

HUD and UI use styled themed widgets with dark-fantasy chrome: gold display has coin icons, monster roster has illustrated cards, and menus have dungeon-stone backgrounds. Score 0 if UI elements are unstyled Godot defaults. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Animation and atmosphere: monsters idle and patrol, heroes walk and fight, traps animate when triggered, digging shows debris, and the dungeon has ambient effects (flickering torches, dripping water). Score 0 if all elements are static. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
